mnemonics.title: Scheduling Criteria
mnemonics.content: CUT TW (CPU UTILIZATION - keep CPU busy, THROUGHPUT - processes/unit time, TURNAROUND TIME - submission to completion, WAITING TIME - time in ready queue, RESPONSE TIME - first response)

mnemonics.title: Scheduler Types
mnemonics.content: LONG TERM = ADMISSION (controls multiprogramming), SHORT TERM = CPU (selects ready process), MEDIUM TERM = SWAPPING (memory management)

mnemonics.title: SJF Minimizes Waiting
mnemonics.content: SHORT JOB FIRST = MINIMUM WAITING TIME (optimal for known burst times)

acronyms.usage: Selects shortest burst time process, minimizes average waiting
Time-sharing scheduling with fixed time quantum
Real-time scheduling based on deadline urgency

visualTips.visualization: Think of the ready queue as people standing in line at a ticket counter. FCFS is like first person in line gets tickets first. SJF is like finding the quickest transaction person first. Round Robin is like each person gets 2 minutes then goes to back of line.
Picture a three-stage filter: Long-term at top (decides who enters building), Medium-term in middle (moves people in/out of waiting room), Short-term at bottom (decines who gets the seat now).
Think of two people reading the same book. One stops, bookmarks their page, gives book to another who opens to their bookmarked page and continues.

analogies.analogy: Like a university admissions office that decides how many students to admit each year to maintain quality and resources.
Like a teacher in a classroom deciding which student to call on next from those with raised hands.
Like a relay runner handing the baton to the next runner - the actual transfer of responsibility.
Like taking turns in a group project where everyone gets equal time to work, then passes to next person.
Like a doctor treating the patient with most urgent/shortest issue first in an emergency room.
Like a job promotion system where hard workers move up but those who slack get demoted to easier tasks.
